finding_results.notes: Recomposed the tip exchange so Miriam's inference comes from the diner scene's service-work texture rather than the style-bible sample skeleton.
finding_results.summary: The tip beat now turns on Miriam reading waitress habit in the gesture of the money, with no close echo of the forbidden calibration line.

finding_results.notes: Made the administrative chain explicit: Mars civil identity handles the pull because Faye's current registry is on Mars, current biometrics plus voice/signature establish her as the live claimant, and revival intake did not surface the audit because sealed misuse reviews stay locked unless requested or still active.
finding_results.summary: The records-office reveal now has an on-page causal path for jurisdiction, claimant eligibility, and the missing revival paperwork.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_07.md:149, chapters/chapter_07.md:153, chapters/chapter_07.md:259, chapters/chapter_07.md:297, chapters/chapter_07.md:333, chapters/chapter_07.md:433, chapters/chapter_07.md:515, chapters/chapter_07.md:525, chapters/chapter_07.md:553
finding_results.notes: Miriam's voice now carries waitress-specific vocabulary in at least three lines: 'stiff you' and 'make trouble for the dealer' (line 149), 'bigger orders, showing off for the table' (line 153), and 'done a shift for tips' (line 259, preserved from snapshot). The clerk's voice now carries bureaucrat-specific vocabulary in at least four lines: 'cryo audits route through the claimant's current civil registry' (line 333, restored from snapshot), 'file a formal objection to the audit language, there's a process for that' (line 433), 'standard classification / credential misuse covers everything from a one-time fraud' (line 515), and 'system logs access events / investigative referral, and that goes through a different office' (line 525). The Miriam scene closer (lines 295-301) now ends in physical interruption — kitchen bell cuts Miriam off, Faye leaves without waiting, parting words are fragmentary and half-heard through closing glass — replacing the neat explanatory closer. The clerk's former aphorisms ('If you want outrage,' 'Why costs extra,' 'Not to the institution / just making me late') have been replaced with procedural scope-limiting responses that could not be swapped with Miriam's service-floor diction. Miriam's 'Goddammit — hold on' (line 297) is profanity rooted in mid-shift pressure; the clerk's 'That's the system' (line 559) is institutional deflection. The two registers no longer converge.

Reworked all three anchored realization beats so they no longer resolve through the same explicit negation/correction template. The first now lands through Miriam's worn-face image, the second through visible action as the room falls off the other woman, and the third through concrete bureaucratic survival imagery.
finding_results.summary: The chapter no longer stacks three key realizations in the same 'Not X. Just Y.' mold.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the diner exchange, made the records-office access logic explicit, and compressed the after-hours coda so the chapter lands on sharper identity vertigo with less redundancy.

Single finding addressed: dialogue register flattening between Miriam (diner scene) and the records clerk (office scene). Seven edits differentiated the two voices by grounding Miriam in service-work vocabulary (stiffing, table dynamics, tipping culture, shift interruptions) and the clerk in bureaucratic procedure (audit classifications, filing processes, investigative referrals, system logs). Replaced three clerk aphorisms that read as authored mini-speeches with institution-shaped deflections. Broke the Miriam scene's neat philosophical closer with a kitchen interruption that leaves the parting unresolved. Preserved all existing alive dialogue (the tip exchange, the scar testimony, the collarbone demonstration, the bathroom scene) and the chapter's reflection section. No continuity changes. Heading contract preserved.
Revised Chapter 7's repeated negation/correction prose tic with varied sentence shapes and concrete imagery while preserving Faye's identity-fracture continuity and the existing scene architecture.
